rspStmt.AuthEnty: Research Unit on the Economics of Excisable Products

verStmt.version: v1.8: Edited, anonymised data for public distribution
verStmt.notes: Rounds 1-7 of the ACP were collected between 2016 and 2018. They were originally released separately in version 1 of the data. The merged Round 1-7 data was released as version 1.2 Round 8 was undertaken between June and August 2019 and the updated data was released as version 1.3: Round 9 was undertaken between November 2019 and Febuary 2020 and the updated data was released as version 1.4 Round 10 was undertaken in 2021 and the updated data released as version 1.5 Rounds 11-12 were undertaken in 2022 and the updated data was released as version 1.6 Rounds 13-15 were undertaken in 2022, 2023, and 2024 and the updated data was released as version 1.7 Rounds 16-17 were undertaken in 2024 and 2025 and the updated data was released as version 1.8

stdyInfo.abstract: The Data was collected by the Research Unit on the Economics of Excisable Products at the University of Cape Town. The project collects prices of cigarettes sold at retail outlets and from street vendors in a number of African countries. The aim is to create a retail cigarette price dataset to enable researchers to make estimations about price differences in these countries, between brands, in urban and rural settlements, and over time. Countries in which the data was collected are: Botswana, Cameroon, Chad, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Ghana, Kenya, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Mauritius, Mozambique, Namibia, Nigeria, South Africa, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia, and Zimbabwe. Not all countries are covered in all rounds.

sumDscr.geogCover: The countries included in the study are: Botswana, Cameroon, Chad, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Ghana, Kenya, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Mauritius, Mozambique, Namibia, Nigeria, South Africa, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia, and Zimbabwe. Not all countries are covered in all rounds.
sumDscr.geogUnit: The data was collected at the level of retailer (often including GPS coordinates of the retail outlet). However, the lowest level of geographic detail available in the public use dataset is Suburb.
sumDscr.universe: The study covered cigarette prices of retailers (including street vendors) in selected African countries.

stdyInfo.notes: The study collected data on prices of cigarettes from retail outlets and street vendors in several African countries.

dataColl.dataCollector: Research Unit on the Economics of Excisable Products (formerly Economics of Tobacco Control Project)
dataColl.collSitu: For this project, the Research Unit on the Economics of Excisable Products organised for University of Cape Town students from different African countries to visit retail outlets and street vendors in their countries, where cigarettes were sold, and obtained permission to take photographs of cigarette packages using their cellphones. Fieldworkers working in the same areas were put in contact to avoid duplication of effort. As such, it is reasonable to assume that duplication was largely avoided and that a store being interviewed twice in one round occured quite rarely if at all. Students were somewhat self-governing in their choice of areas to visit and stores from which to gather data. How the student groups covering an area divided up that area has not been well documented. As such, there may be geographic bias, for example against poorer areas that are more difficult to interview. This also means that a given interviewer might repeat whatever bias/mistakes they tend to make over all the rounds (if they remained on the project) making any bias hard to detect. In some cases the same store (retailer/spaza shop/street vendor) was interviewed in more than one round of data collection.
dataColl.actMin: The Project received ethics approval for data collection from the Ethics Committee of the Faculty of Commerce at the University of Cape Town on the 8th of October 2015.
dataColl.cleanOps: Data collected by REEP was in excel format and not serialised. Datafirst has serialised and cleaned the data to create a research-ready series. Appending the rounds required harmonising variable names. The brand names (eg Marborough) and sub-brands (eg gold) required significant cleaning due to typos and errors in data collection. Some observations with bad values were dropped from the final dataset. DataFirst has merged the data from all rounds.

useStmt.citReq: Research Unit on the Economics of Excisable Products. African Cigarette Prices 2016-2025 [dataset]. Version 1.8. Cape Town: REEP [producer], 2025. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2025. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/HB2J-K996
useStmt.conditions: Public access data for use under a Creative Commons CC-BY (Attribution-only) License

catgry.labl: Taxi Rank At The Railway Yard (Cbd)
Â Samora Machel & Dr A B Xuma St
Main North 1, Fribel, Main South Road, Cathedral Area

var.name: Outlet_Type
var.labl: Outlet Type - Retailer, Spaza Shop or Street Vendor

var.name: Retail_Subtype
var.labl: Retail Outlet Subtypes (Rounds 4-8 only, retail outlets only)

var.name: Product
var.labl: Type of Tobacco Product (Eg cigarette, snuff, cigar, Rounds 4-8 Only)

var.name: Quantity
var.labl: Number of cigarettes in pack (1 if single)

var.name: Unusual_Quantity_Flg
var.labl: The quantity of cigarettes sold is flagged as unusual

var.name: Dollar_Price_Pack_Cigarettes
var.labl: Dollar Price for a standard pack of 20 (=price per cig*20)

catgry.catValu: Bulk Pack In Plastic Wrap (Checked Photo)
Can'T Find Pics, Assume Normal Quantity
Can'T See The Quantity (Could Be 10) But Didn'T Want To Change
Changed To 1 (Was Written Incorrectly As 20 Pack)
Changed To Correct Mass Sale Quantities
Changed To Correct Price According To Photo
Changed To Snuff From Cigarettes (Checked Photo)
Checked Photo, Correct Price And Quantity
Checked Photom (Says 2 So Could Be 2*10 Pack) - Would Make Sense
Checked With Photo, Was A Special
Cigarette Sticks In A Jar. Sub-Brand Unknown
Different To The Pipe Tobacco Brand (Not An Error)
Difficult to confirm the product type and quantity due to image quality
Exchange rate taken from the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe
Have Checked These Quantities/Prices And They Are Correct
No Photo - Googled And Assumed To Be Snuff (Grams)
No Photo, Assumed Quanities Based On Prices From The Same Fieldworker
Photo Unlear Assumed To Be Barath
Price List Of Brands. Sub-Brands Not Included
Pricelist With Brands Only. No Sub-Brands
Seems To Be A Different Brand To Nyasa 2010
Store Front Photo. Price List With Brands Only. No Sub-Brands
Sub-Brand Can Not Be Seen In The Photo
The 20 Percent Extra Represents A Longer Cigarrete
The Name Of This Brand Was Changed To "Portsman"
The Photo Has A Pricelist Of Brands Only. Sub-Brands Are Not Included
The Quantity In The Image Is Incorrect. It Should Be 20 And Not 200. The Fieldworker Was Consulted
These Are All Singles With Price = 4 (Not 40)

var.name: Quantity_Other
var.labl: Quantity Other Tobacco Products (rams or unit)
